classificationPolicy.acceptable_product_code: Static product-code SEO remains acceptable when the page family is not CMS-backed editorial authority.
classificationPolicy.safe_cms_backed_fallback: Fallback is acceptable when CMS absence produces noindex, notFound, or minimal shell behavior without indexable frontend editorial SEO authority.
classificationPolicy.watchlist: Frontend deterministic rendering uses CMS visible content but can still become a second SEO/schema authority if backend seo.surface.v1 coverage weakens.
classificationPolicy.migration_required: Frontend fallback can affect indexable SEO/GEO exposure and must be governed before Topic Graph, pSEO, or GEO expansion.

surfaces.frontendFallback: CMS fetch failure returns noindex metadata or a minimal shell instead of full local editorial SEO authority.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Keep noindex/minimal-shell fallback; ensure future homepage content changes remain CMS-owned.

surfaces.frontendFallback: CMS fetch failure returns noindex/minimal shell behavior; category content is still rendered from typed product surface when CMS is unavailable.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Before expanding test category SEO, verify category metadata is backend-authoritative and no frontend editorial fallback can become indexable.

surfaces.frontendFallback: CMS fetch failure returns noindex metadata and CareerMinimalShell.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Keep fallback noindex-only; do not add local career hub copy as publishable SEO content.

surfaces.cmsAuthority: CMS Article + Article SEO + last-known-good cache
surfaces.frontendFallback: If Article SEO JSON-LD is absent, frontend builds Article JSON-LD from CMS article title, excerpt, dates, and author.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Move Article JSON-LD completeness into backend seo.surface.v1 before article or GEO expansion; keep current fallback only as compatibility.

surfaces.frontendFallback: Empty CMS response renders an empty state while metadata is deterministic hub metadata.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Accept empty-state fallback; do not add local article lists or article SEO copies.

surfaces.frontendFallback: Frontend renders backend normalized topic JSON-LD plus deterministic WebPage, BreadcrumbList, and FAQPage from visible CMS content.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Before Topic Graph rollout, define which structured data keys are backend-owned and which deterministic visible-content schemas remain frontend-rendered.

surfaces.frontendFallback: CMS absence renders an empty CMS state with deterministic hub WebPage/Breadcrumb JSON-LD.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Keep as read-only hub until Topic Graph governance defines indexable expansion criteria.

surfaces.cmsAuthority: CMS career guide + career guide SEO
surfaces.frontendFallback: Frontend normalizes CMS guide SEO and renders breadcrumb JSON-LD locally.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Before career guide SEO expansion, require backend schema completeness for guide detail pages and keep frontend breadcrumb rendering deterministic only.

surfaces.frontendFallback: Metadata falls back from seoTitle/metaDescription to CMS title/summary; page absence becomes noindex or notFound.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Accept CMS-field fallback; keep static policy/help content in backend content_pages.

surfaces.frontendFallback: llms routes fall back to a stable public topic set when the topics CMS is unavailable.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Before Topic Graph expansion, replace fallback topic enumeration with backend exposure authority or an explicitly versioned compatibility fixture.

surfaces.cmsAuthority: Hybrid CMS personality profile plus product-code MBTI compatibility
surfaces.frontendFallback: MBTI personality content can use product-code compatibility content for legacy profile rendering.
surfaces.recommendedAction: Accept as product-code compatibility, but do not expand personality SEO graph until CMS authority is explicit.

blockedBeforeExpansion: Article detail JSON-LD fallback must be reconciled with backend seo.surface.v1 before article GEO expansion.
llms topic fallback enumeration must be governed before Topic Graph rollout.
Topic detail structured-data ownership must be documented before adding topic clusters.
